welcome.label: Welcome
welcome.heading: Let's get you set up in ~5 minutes
welcome.sub: Realtime Translate translates your Google Meet calls in real time. Here's how it works:
welcome.diagramDirA: You speak in Portuguese, your contact hears English
welcome.diagramDirB: Your contact speaks English, you hear Portuguese

key.label: OpenAI Key
key.heading: Your OpenAI key
key.sub: Each user brings their own key. Cost: ~$0.30 per 5 minutes of conversation.
key.signupLink: I don't have a key — take me to OpenAI signup
key.howToToggle: How do I get a key?
key.howToStep3: Create an API key (API Keys → Create new secret key)

cables.label: Virtual cables
cables.detectedSub: Ready to route audio between you and Meet.
cables.missingHeading: You need to install VB-CABLE A+B
cables.missingSub: It's a pair of virtual audio cables. Third-party software (donationware), safe and widely used.
cables.rescanFailToast: Didn't detect it. Did you reboot after installing?
cables.installStep1: Download the ZIP from the VB-Audio site
cables.installStep2: Extract the contents to a folder

devices.label: Devices
devices.heading: Your audio devices
devices.sub: Select which mic and headset you use.

meet.label: Configure Meet
meet.heading: Configure Google Meet
meet.sub: This step is manual — we can't verify it automatically, but it's quick.
meet.step2: Click the 3 dots in the bottom-right corner → Settings
meet.step5: Speakers: select 'CABLE-B Input (VB-Audio Cable B)' (NOT the '16ch' one)

test.label: Test translation
test.heading: Test translation
test.sub: Let's validate everything works with 2 short calls to OpenAI (~$0.10 total). You can skip if you prefer.
test.directionAExplain: App will speak a Portuguese phrase, translate to English, and validate that the cable receives it.
test.directionBExplain: App will speak an English phrase, translate to Portuguese, and play it on your headset.
test.skipWarning: Translation may fail on the first call if something is misconfigured.
